idea_candidates.name: Increment Navigation Buttons
idea_candidates.summary: Add buttons to navigate lists by specific increments (e.g., 5 or 10 lines) for quicker access to desired items.
idea_candidates.proposed_solution: Implement buttons or shortcuts that allow users to jump by predefined increments within lists, reducing the need for manual scrolling.
idea_candidates.value_proposition: Saves time and reduces frustration for users who frequently navigate large lists, improving overall productivity.
idea_candidates.differentiation: Unlike traditional scrollbars, this feature provides precise control over list navigation, catering to specific user needs.
idea_candidates.revenue_model: Improved user satisfaction and retention, leading to increased usage of the inventory system.
idea_candidates.feasibility: Technically feasible with minimal development effort, as it involves adding UI elements and backend logic for increment-based navigation.
risks: Potential UI clutter if not designed thoughtfully
User confusion if increments are not clearly labeled

idea_candidates.name: Alphabetical Sorting for Lists
idea_candidates.summary: Implement alphabetical sorting for dropdown lists to facilitate quicker selection of items, such as communities or categories.
idea_candidates.proposed_solution: Ensure all dropdown lists, especially those for selecting communities or categories, are sorted alphabetically by default.
idea_candidates.value_proposition: Makes it easier for users to find specific items in long lists, reducing search time and improving user experience.
idea_candidates.differentiation: Standardizes list organization, aligning with user expectations and best practices for list navigation.
idea_candidates.revenue_model: Enhanced user satisfaction and efficiency, contributing to higher system adoption and retention.
idea_candidates.feasibility: Straightforward implementation, requiring updates to list rendering logic and backend data sorting.
risks: Potential performance impact for very large lists
Need for consistent sorting across all lists
